algebra_primitives.description: Kleene three-valued truth domain {T,F,U}; a direct-sum component of V, disjoint from numbers.
Strong Kleene meet (greatest lower bound) on K3; the algebraic primitive AND derives from.
Strong Kleene join (least upper bound) on K3; the algebraic primitive OR derives from.
Order-reversing involution on K3 fixing U; the algebraic primitive NOT derives from.
Bihomographic (Gosper) transform over exact reals; ADD/SUB/MUL/DIV are coefficient instances.
Budgeted exact-real ordering; decides <, =, > within a budget or yields the logical U.
Continued-fraction construction of exact reals; canonical irrational observation, not an approximation.
Gosper normalization over continued fractions; exact-real combination without rational fallback.
Text value domain as a codepoint sequence, tracked separately from numeric scalar arithmetic.
Operational absence/Bubble domain for NIL values with structured reasons; disjoint from FALSE, zero, and logical UNKNOWN.
Bubble monad pass-through: an operational NIL with reason propagates through a derived word unchanged.
Bubble/NIL handler that replaces operational absence with a fallback while preserving present values.
Finite ordered index structure for vector values; GET/LENGTH/TAKE/SPLIT/CONCAT/REVERSE/RANGE/REORDER/COLLECT derive from this sequence algebra.
Functorial/applicative lift of a scalar operation over a tensor index structure (map).
zipWith lift of a scalar binary operation with documented broadcast rules.
Shape/index isomorphisms for tensors; RESHAPE, TRANSPOSE, SHAPE, RANK, and FILL derive from this reshape-group action.
Consumption policy that preserves operands; one axis of the modifier combinator.
Consumption policy that removes operands after applying a word; one axis of the modifier combinator.
Region selection over the whole operand stack; one axis of the modifier combinator.
Region selection over the top stack item; one axis of the modifier combinator.
Combinator that transforms a base state transformer (region x consumption x reconstruction).
Monoid composition of state transformers over Sigma = Stack x Dict x Eff.
Identity state transformer over Sigma = Stack x Dict x Eff; IDLE and no-op sugar derive from it.
Deterministic dictionary lookup / name resolution over Dict.
Finite partial map with insertion order; the value-domain component records inhabit.
Append into the free-monoid effect log Eff, the third component of Sigma.
Structured diagnostic observation; never a leaked host/Rust error variant.
Capability gate guarding a hosted effect; failure is a structured diagnostic, not a Core value.
Child-runtime/supervisor handle value domain; currently Exploratory, outside the Core contract.

entries.notes: Boolean value; distinct from numeric 1/1.
Boolean value; distinct from numeric 0/1.
Primitive operational absence; distinct from FALSE, numeric zero, and logical UNKNOWN. Reasoned NIL/Bubble values propagate through NIL-passthrough words.
Strong Kleene conjunction over TRUE/FALSE/UNKNOWN.
Strong Kleene disjunction.
Strong Kleene negation.
Exact rational arithmetic; division by zero observes NIL rather than host exceptions.
Exact-real addition as a bihomographic arithmetic word; NIL operands bubble through unchanged.
Exact-real subtraction as a bihomographic arithmetic word; NIL operands bubble through unchanged.
Exact-real multiplication as a bihomographic arithmetic word; includes arbitrary-precision integer observations.
Exact-real division as a bihomographic arithmetic word; division by zero projects to NIL rather than a host exception.
Modulo is derived as x - floor(x/y)·y over exact reals, so it depends on integer projection plus exact arithmetic and preserves Bubble/NIL absence.
FLOOR — exact-real integer projection toward negative infinity. Projecting failures create NIL/Bubble observations.
CEIL — exact-real integer projection toward positive infinity. Projecting failures create NIL/Bubble observations.
ROUND — exact-real nearest-integer projection without float round trips. Projecting failures create NIL/Bubble observations.
Decided comparisons produce Boolean observations; budgeted equality can produce UNKNOWN.
Budgeted exact-real equality projected into the K3 truth domain; NIL operands pass through unchanged.
Inequality is the K3-domain complement of budgeted equality when decided, with UNKNOWN and NIL preserved by projection/passthrough.
Budgeted exact-real strict less-than projected into the K3 truth domain; undecidable comparisons yield UNKNOWN.
Less-than-or-equal is derived from the same budgeted order observation as LT/EQ, projected into K3 truth.
Greater-than is the operand-dual of LT over the shared budgeted-order primitive.
Greater-than-or-equal is the operand-dual of LTE over the shared budgeted-order primitive.
Surface exposure of the explicit budgeted-order primitive: returns -1/0/1 for decided order or UNKNOWN with agreedPrefix on budget exhaustion.
Irrational observations are canonical continued fractions, never sqrt(...) Debug-style wrappers or ~ rational approximations.
Consumption modifier that preserves operands and pushes the result.
Region modifier selecting the whole operand stack; conformance guards the previous underflow regression.
Top-of-stack region modifier; selects the singleton top region for the next state-transformer word.
Operand-consuming modifier; selects the consume/eat policy for the next state-transformer word.
No-op pipeline marker; observationally the identity state transformer.
Bubble/NIL coalescing handler; replaces operational absence with a fallback and keeps present values.
Identity control word over the interpreter state transformer.
K3-honest guarded state-transformer choice; UNKNOWN guards do not fire.
Executes a code value by reflecting it into state-transformer composition.
Parses source text and reflects the resulting program into state-transformer composition.
Functorial/applicative lift of a block over an indexed sequence.
Predicate restriction over an indexed sequence using a K3 truth-valued block.
Catamorphism over an indexed sequence with an explicit accumulator and combiner state transformer.
Anamorphism that generates an indexed sequence by repeatedly applying a state transition.
Existential predicate fold over an indexed sequence into the K3 truth domain.
Universal predicate fold over an indexed sequence into the K3 truth domain.
Counting fold over the predicate-satisfying elements of an indexed sequence.
Prefix-catamorphism exposing intermediate accumulator states over an indexed sequence.
Clock reads are hosted capabilities with deterministic conformance host observations.
CSPRNG observations are host-provided and capability-gated.
Serial effects are outside Core but have protocol-level observation contracts.
Capability failures are structured diagnostics, not leaked Rust error variants.
Structural vector and tensor observations, including shape/rank and broadcast arithmetic.
Indexed projection over vectors/tensors; out-of-range indices project to NIL/Bubble while preserving the source vector observation.
Point insertion as an ordered-sequence transform over the vector index structure.
Point replacement as an ordered-sequence transform over the vector index structure.
Point removal as an ordered-sequence transform; removing the last element projects to NIL/Bubble.
Cardinality observation over the indexed-sequence structure.
Prefix/suffix projection over the indexed-sequence structure.
Chunking an indexed sequence into ordered subsequences.
Free-monoid concatenation of ordered vector sequences.
Involutive order reversal on indexed sequences.
Deterministic construction of an inclusive integer indexed sequence.
Permutation action over an indexed vector sequence.
Stack-to-vector collection that constructs an indexed sequence from the top N stack values.
Shape observation for a tensor/indexed sequence.
Rank observation: the dimension count of a tensor/indexed sequence.
Reshape-group action that changes tensor shape without changing element order or count.
Axis-swap action in the tensor reshape group; 2-D transpose is an involution.
Tensor constructor that fills a requested shape with a repeated value.
Record laws exist, but conformance still needs representative record observations before marking Formalized here.
String algebra has law coverage; conformance still needs stable encoding/role cases.
Display-role retagging into canonical continued-fraction serialization; it preserves the underlying exact value.
Splits a text value into the finite codepoint sequence of one-character text values.
Folds a vector of text/codepoint fragments back into one text value.
Whitespace-normalization endomorphism over codepoint sequences; idempotent at fixed points.
Left-sided whitespace-normalization endomorphism over codepoint sequences.
Right-sided whitespace-normalization endomorphism over codepoint sequences.
Splits a codepoint sequence by a separator sequence into ordered text parts.
Codepoint-sequence replacement transform over all occurrences of a substring.
Prefix predicate over codepoint sequences, projected into the K3 truth domain.
Suffix predicate over codepoint sequences, projected into the K3 truth domain.
Parses text/codepoint sequences into exact numbers; malformed numeric text projects to NIL/Bubble.
Renders a value into canonical text/codepoint-sequence form under the text role.
Parses or projects a value into the K3 truth domain.
Maps an integer codepoint into a one-character text value; invalid codepoints project to NIL/Bubble.
Parses or projects a value into the K3 truth domain.
Maps an integer codepoint into a one-character text value; invalid codepoints project to NIL/Bubble.
User dictionaries and module imports are deterministic name-resolution observations.
Child-runtime semantics are modeled but not yet part of the Core portability contract.
Surface sugar for ADD; desugaring is observation-transparent.
Surface sugar for SUB; desugaring is observation-transparent.
Surface sugar for MUL; desugaring is observation-transparent.
Surface sugar for MOD; desugaring is observation-transparent.
Surface sugar for EQ; desugaring is observation-transparent.
Surface sugar for LT; desugaring is observation-transparent.
Surface sugar for LTE; desugaring is observation-transparent.
Surface sugar for GT; desugaring is observation-transparent.
Surface sugar for GTE; desugaring is observation-transparent.
Surface sugar for NEQ; desugaring is observation-transparent.
Surface sugar for FORC; desugaring is observation-transparent.
Surface sugar for AND; desugaring is observation-transparent.
Surface sugar for TOP; desugaring is observation-transparent.
Surface sugar for STAK; desugaring is observation-transparent.
Surface sugar for EAT; desugaring is observation-transparent.
Surface sugar for KEEP; desugaring is observation-transparent.
Editor/input helper marker; not a runtime coreword.
Surface sugar for LOOKUP; desugaring is observation-transparent.
Surface sugar for PIPE; desugaring is observation-transparent.
